description_long: This object is a pine cone, characterized by its overlapping, woody scales arranged in a spiral pattern around a central core. The scales are dark brown on the exterior, with a rough, textured surface and some lighter, weathered areas on the base. The cone is ovoid in shape, tapering slightly at one end where the stem remnant is visible. The scales are thick and rigid, with some edges appearing slightly chipped or irregular, typical of natural tree debris. The overall structure is dense, with little visible space between the scales, and the object appears solid and robust. No leaves or green material are present, and the surface is entirely covered by the woody scales.
description: A dense, brown pine cone with overlapping woody scales and a rough, textured surface. The object is ovoid and solid, with a visible stem remnant at one end.
description_view_0: The pine cone is seen from the side, showing the layered, dark brown scales and the open, irregular tips.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the pointed end and the dense arrangement of scales, with the stem remnant partially visible.
description_view_2: The base of the pine cone is visible, showing the tightly packed, lighter-colored scales and the central core.
description_view_3: The view focuses on the rounded base, emphasizing the compact, overlapping scales and the absence of open tips.
